titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/223

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=174 status=live modified=2017-02-15 19:15:43.717584+00 | translation moa=175 status=live modified=2017-02-15 19:15:46.374368+00

div.p:
Thor’ filius Swani . Omnibus amicis suis & dominis salutem. Sciant tam posteri quam presentes me consilio & concessu heredum meorum dedisse & concessisse pro
salute anime mee & antecessorum meorum in perpetuam elemosinam deo & ęcclesię sancte crucis de Edenesburc & fratribus ibidem deo seruientibus . totum illud
donationis quod uidebar habere in ęcclesia de Treuernent in terris . & pascuis . & decimis . libere & quiete ab omni seculari dominatione & exactione .
& preter illa quę antecessores mei predicte ęcclesię de Treuernent contulerunt  do & concedo . & per presentem cartam confirmo predicte ęcclesię duas
domos cum duabus toftis . predictis canonicis cum prefata ęcclesia in perpetuam elemosinam confirmatas . filiis meis mecum predicta omnia conce
dentibus & confirmantibus . His testibus Willelmo Morauiensi episcopo . Osberno abbate de Eaddeuurd’ . Thor’ archidiacono . Aiolf decano . Nicholao cler’ regis .
Neis filio Chilieri . Ædmund’ filio forn . Bernardo filio Tocce . Gilandrea eius dapifero . Ædmundo de fageside . Alden

Thor son of Swain to all his friends and lords, Greeting: Let those to come as well as those present know that I, by the counsel and consent of my heirs, have given and granted, for the salvation of my soul and my ancestors’, in perpetual alms to God and the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and the brethren serving God there, the whole of the donation that I was seen to have in the church of Tranent in lands and pastures and teinds, free and quit from every secular dominion and exaction. And besides those things that my ancestors bestowed to the aforesaid church of Tranent, I give and grant, and by the present charter make firm, to the said church two dwellings with two tofts to the aforesaid canons with the aforementioned church confirmed in perpetual alms, my sons granting and confirming all things aforesaid with me. With these witnesses, William bishop of Moray, Osbern abbot of Jedburgh, Thorald the archdeacon, Aiulf the dean, Nicholas the king’s clerk, Ness son of Chilierus, Edmund son of Forn, Bernard son of Tóki, Gillanders his steward, Edmund of Fazeside, Aldan.
